 {"id":"bd-545","title":"Math retirement gate: deprecate unused/non-beneficial modules with CI enforcement","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- Add CI rule: modules lacking decision linkage + measurable benefit cannot stay in production manifest.
- Create staged deprecation path and migration notes.

Acceptance:
- Production code size and hot-path complexity decrease measurably.
- Removed modules remain available under research feature if needed.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): math_retirement_test (9 pass) + gate script. Stages ordered, RC1 candidates match governance, migration waves cover all RC1, production-compliant match governance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.600562341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Math retirement gate operational. 9 harness tests pass. check_math_retirement.sh validates deprecation stages, RC1 candidates, migration waves, and waivers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","ci","critique","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-545","depends_on_id":"bd-24x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-545","depends_on_id":"bd-rqn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-5ajp","title":"EPIC: Bugs found and fixed in comprehensive audit","description":"This epic tracks all bugs found and fixed during the comprehensive multi-round code audit. These are ALREADY FIXED — this epic serves as documentation and regression tracking.

TOTAL BUGS FIXED: 30+

CRITICAL FIXES (caused crashes):
- dl_iterate_phdr was a no-op stub → now delegates to host
- fopen/fdopen returned fake stream IDs → now delegate to host for real FILE*
- _IO_2_1_{stdin,stdout,stderr}_ shadowed host with zeros → removed exports
- setjmp/longjmp were abort stubs → now delegate to host
- on_exit handlers never called during exit() → now invoked in reverse order
- resolve_host_symbol_raw infinite recursion → fixed bootstrap ordering

HIGH FIXES (data corruption/incorrect behavior):
- iconv_abi apply_progress usize underflow → saturating_sub
- stdlib_abi gcvt/qgcvt negative ndigit → max(0) guard
- malloc_abi is_bump_ptr false positives → added magic sentinel check
- malloc_abi calloc reentrant overflow → checked_mul
- stdio_abi fprintf/vfprintf/setvbuf missing host delegation
- HashSlot missing #[repr(C)] → cast to Entry could read wrong fields
- pthread_cancel self-cancellation never triggers → calls pthread_testcancel

MEDIUM FIXES (errno/correctness):
- termios cfsetispeed/cfsetospeed missing errno
- signal_abi raise() missing errno
- wchar_abi mbtowc/wctomb missing EILSEQ errno
- unistd_abi read/write missing EFAULT errno
- fortify __getlogin_r_chk/__ttyname_r_chk ignoring nreal
- c11threads thrd_sleep using raw __errno_location
- poll_abi sched_yield missing errno
- nftw/fts_open using libc::lstat instead of raw syscall

LOW FIXES (code quality):
- resource_abi unnecessary Box allocation
- inet_abi hard-coded struct sizes → size_of/offset_of
- glibc_internal_abi redundant as isize as usize cast","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.813375546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.813375546Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.813375546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5fw","title":"Completion Program: Final release-exit contract + deterministic closure protocol","description":"Background:
- Current project state has strong partial coverage and many gates, but final completion is still fragmented across open epics and in-progress subsystem tasks.
- We need one deterministic closure contract so future execution and review do not rely on implied assumptions.

Goal:
- Define and enforce the exact end-state required to claim “finished, correct, and optimized” for the current declared replacement level.

Deliverables:
1) Explicit release-exit checklist for L0 (interpose) with objective pass/fail checks.
2) Staged upgrade requirements for L1/L2 progression (what must be true before claim changes).
3) Artifact bundle spec for every release candidate:
   - conformance reports,
   - reality report,
   - replacement/profile reports,
   - perf baselines + deltas,
   - evidence/log indices.
4) Gate ordering and ownership (which scripts/tests run in what order and why).

Acceptance Criteria:
- A clean checkout can run a single scripted release dry-run and produce a complete pass/fail dossier.
- No “manual interpretation” required to determine readiness.
- Any missing artifact or breached threshold fails deterministically with actionable diagnostics.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Add unit tests for checklist parsing/validation logic.
- Add end-to-end release-dry-run script tests (strict and hardened where relevant).
- Emit structured run logs including trace_id, mode, gate_name, outcome, errno/exit_code, timing, and artifact paths.

Optimization/Proof Requirements (Extreme Optimization skill):
- Include benchmark baseline references for all release-critical hot paths.
- Require isomorphism proof references for any optimization included in the release window.

Porting/Spec-first Requirements (Porting-to-Rust skill):
- Reference behavior contracts from PLAN/EXISTING/PROPOSED/FEATURE_PARITY docs directly in acceptance checks so closure is specification-backed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.154973750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:18:04.920565120Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:18:04.920450105Z","close_reason":"EPIC complete: all 5 children closed. Release closure contract, gate DAG, dossier validator, proof binder, and branch diversity gate all delivered.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","release","roadmap","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1024,"issue_id":"bd-5fw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning update (Codex):

- Expanded `bd-2uro` with a self-contained North Star + non-negotiable contracts + phase map + proof-obligation index (so we do not need to consult PLAN_TO_PORT_GLIBC_TO_RUST.md for closure intent).
- Added new child `bd-5fw.4` (Proof Obligations Binder) and set `bd-5fw.3` to *block on* it. This is intended to make release-dossier requirements mechanical: theorem -> evidence -> gate mapping.
- Code audit note (from explorer): README’s healing-action list appears to mention `QuarantineStale`, but `frankenlibc-membrane::HealingAction` does not define it. Also, membrane currently refuses to compile without `runtime-math-production` feature. These should be treated as doc-claim drift and covered by `bd-3rw.5` doc drift gates.

Coordination:
- MCP Agent Mail is currently overloaded (`health_level=red`, as of 2026-02-12). Using bead comments for now.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:49:34Z"},{"id":1025,"issue_id":"bd-5fw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ALL CHILDREN CLOSED: bd-5fw.1 (closure contract), bd-5fw.2 (gate DAG), bd-5fw.3 (dossier validator), bd-5fw.4 (proof binder), bd-5fw.5 (branch diversity gate). EPIC deliverables complete: release-exit checklist, gate ordering, artifact bundle spec, dossier validator. Closing EPIC. —PearlFinch","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:18:04Z"}]}
